title: If/Else Statements 🔀
description: Make your app take different paths depending on conditions.

slides.title: Making Decisions 🚦
slides.content:
# The Crossroads

Until now, every line of code we wrote gave a single result.
But real apps need to make decisions!

If the user is logged in, show the home page. If not, show the login screen.

We do this using **`if` statements**, the most powerful tool in programming.

slides.title: The `if` block 🧱

quizData.question: What happens if the condition inside the `if()` statement is false?

options.text: Dart runs the code block anyway but warns you.
Dart skips over the curly {} brace block entirely.

quizData.explanation: An 'if' block ONLY executes when the condition is strictly true.

slides.title: The `else` Block ↩️
See it in Action
slides.content: Try changing the `isRaining` variable and run it!

quizData.explanation: The else block catches any conditions that fail the preceding 'if'.

slides.title: Multiple Choices: else if 🪜

quizData.question: Can you have multiple `else if` blocks attached to one `if` statement?

options.text: No, only one `else if` is allowed.
Yes, you can chain as many as you need.

quizData.explanation: You can insert as many else-if blocks as required to handle all your different scenarios.
